brand.name: The Local Jewel
brand.description: Custom lab-grown diamond / engagement ring brand transitioning to a robust sales engine.

hierarchy.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-serif tracking-tight leading-none text-[var(--lj-accent)]
hierarchy.h2: text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-serif tracking-tight leading-tight

rules: Use Cormorant Garamond exclusively for large emotional headings (H1, H2, H3).
Use Outfit for all UI elements: buttons, navigation links, prices, product titles, and body copy.
Never center text by default; align left for D2C readability unless in a specific symmetrical hero section.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous spacing. Allow the jewelry photography to breathe.
spacing_and_layout.sections: py-20 md:py-32 px-4 md:px-8 max-w-7xl mx-auto
spacing_and_layout.borders_and_radius: radius-none or extremely subtle (rounded-sm). Luxury jewelry avoids heavily rounded 'tech' corners. Sharp, elegant lines.

header_mega_menu.layout: Sticky header (backdrop-blur-xl bg-[var(--lj-bg)]/90 z-50). Condensed on scroll.
header_mega_menu.desktop_interaction: Navigation links open a full-width or large dropdown. The dropdown contains 2-3 columns of sub-links on the left, and a dedicated image container on the right (w-1/3 or w-80).
header_mega_menu.hover_logic: React state tracks the hovered sub-link. The right-side image smoothly swaps (fade transition) to match the hovered link's preview image.
header_mega_menu.admin_editable: Ensure the menu data structure allows an admin panel to define { label, href, hoverImageUrl } for each link.

product_card.aspect_ratio: aspect-[4/5] for the image container to showcase vertical ring proportions.
product_card.image_behavior: overflow-hidden. Image scales up (hover:scale-105) on card hover with a slow transition (duration-700).
product_card.badges: Absolute positioned top-left. Minimalist rectangular badges (e.g., 'Best Seller', 'New').
product_card.typography: Product name in Outfit medium (text-sm or text-base).
product_card.pricing: Flex row. Current price in bold. Compare-at price in var(--lj-muted) with line-through.
product_card.action: Quick 'Add to Cart' button appearing on hover over the image, or a clean '+' icon button.

product_detail_page.layout: Split screen desktop. Left: Sticky image gallery (grid of images or large main image + thumbnails). Right: Scrollable product info.
product_detail_page.sticky_buy_box: On mobile, a sticky bar at the bottom with Price and 'Add to Cart' to ensure high conversion.
product_detail_page.selectors: Visual swatches for Metal (Gold, Silver, Rose colors in a circle). Pill-shaped buttons for Carat sizes.
product_detail_page.trust_signals: Place trust badges (Free Shipping, Lifetime Warranty, Secure Checkout) directly under the Buy button.

cart_drawer.layout: Slide-in from right. Fixed width (e.g., w-full sm:w-[400px]).
cart_drawer.express_checkout: Prominent Apple Pay / Google Pay dynamic button at the top of the summary area (black/white high contrast) to reduce friction.
cart_drawer.upsell: Include a small 'You might also like' or 'Add a matching band' section within the drawer.

media.guidelines: Images must be high-contrast, sharp, and premium. Use subtle grey or var(--lj-bg) backgrounds for isolated products.

image_urls.description: Elegant model in pink gown wearing jewelry, perfect for emotional hero banners.
Couple holding hands with engagement ring in focus. Use for 'Engagement Rings' section.
Silver diamond ring on dark box. Perfect for product card or mega-menu hover preview.
Exquisite diamond ring on soft blue stand. Good for product highlights.

instructions_to_main_agent: Do NOT use TypeScript (.tsx). Write all components in JavaScript (.js or .jsx).
Apply data-testid attributes to ALL interactive elements (e.g., data-testid='mega-menu-link', data-testid='add-to-cart-btn', data-testid='express-checkout-btn').
Implement the Mega Menu logic: Create a state variable 'activeImage' that updates onMouseEnter of sub-links. Use absolute positioning and opacity transitions for the image swap.
Integrate Shadcn UI elements but heavily customize them to remove the 'tech/Vercel' look. Use square corners (rounded-none) and the provided CSS variables.
Ensure 'lucide-react' is used for icons (e.g., ShoppingBag, Search, Menu, ChevronDown).
For the express checkout, create dummy UI elements that visually represent Apple Pay/Google Pay buttons (black bg, white text, rounded-full) to demonstrate the D2C conversion flow.
The Collections page and Homepage sections must use the mapped CSS variables (--lj-bg, --lj-accent, etc.) to seamlessly blend with the existing lead-gen app.
Do not use generic placeholder image URLs; map the provided URLs from the 'media' section.
